title: 🚑 ABCDE APPROACH
content:
🔴 A – Airway
• Can the patient speak
• Look for obstruction
• Suction if needed
• If trauma is suspected protect cervical spine

🟠 B – Breathing
• Respiratory rate
• Oxygen saturation
• Chest rise
• Work of breathing
• Give oxygen if hypoxic

🟡 C – Circulation
• Pulse
• Blood pressure
• Capillary refill
• Check for bleeding
• IV access

🟢 D – Disability
• GCS
• Pupils
• Blood glucose

🔵 E – Exposure
• Full body check
• Look for hidden trauma, rash, burns, bleeding
• Prevent hypothermia

High-yield rule
• In emergency always stabilize before detailed diagnosis

title: 💥 SHOCK OVERVIEW
content:
🔴 Hypovolemic shock
• Blood loss or fluid loss
• Cold extremities
• Tachycardia
• Low BP

🟠 Septic shock
• Infection source
• Fever or hypothermia
• Warm extremities early
• High lactate

🟡 Cardiogenic shock
• MI or pump failure
• Pulmonary edema may occur
• Cool extremities

🟢 Obstructive shock
• PE
• Cardiac tamponade
• Tension pneumothorax

Exam shortcut
• Cold + empty = hypovolemic
• Warm + infected = septic
• Wet lungs + weak pump = cardiogenic

title: 🦠 SEPSIS BUNDLE
content:
🚨 Suspect sepsis when
• Infection + hypotension
• Fever or hypothermia
• Tachycardia
• Altered mental state
• High lactate

Immediate steps
• ABCs
• Blood cultures if possible
• Broad-spectrum antibiotics early
• IV crystalloids
• Lactate measurement
• Vasopressors if hypotension persists after fluids

High-yield exam point
• Do not delay antibiotics
• Fluids first then vasopressors if needed

title: 💉 ANAPHYLAXIS
content:
Classic clues
• Sudden onset
• Urticaria
• Angioedema
• Wheeze or stridor
• Hypotension
• GI symptoms after allergen exposure

First treatment
• IM epinephrine immediately

Then
• Airway support
• Oxygen
• IV fluids
• Antihistamine as adjunct
• Steroid as adjunct

High-yield rule
• Epinephrine first
• Never delay epinephrine while waiting for rash to worsen

title: ❤️ ACUTE CORONARY SYNDROME
content:
Classic features
• Crushing chest pain
• Radiation to left arm or jaw
• Diaphoresis
• Nausea
• Dyspnea

Immediate steps
• ECG within 10 minutes
• Troponin
• Aspirin unless contraindicated
• Nitroglycerin if appropriate

STEMI
• PCI preferred if rapidly available
• Thrombolysis if PCI unavailable and criteria met

High-yield rule
• Time is myocardium

title: 🫀 CARDIAC ARREST BASICS
content:
If no pulse and no breathing
• Start CPR immediately
• Call for help
• Attach defibrillator

Shockable rhythms
• Ventricular fibrillation
• Pulseless ventricular tachycardia

Non-shockable rhythms
• Asystole
• PEA

Medication
• Epinephrine is commonly used during arrest

High-yield rule
• Early CPR + early defibrillation = best survival chance

title: 🧠 STROKE APPROACH
content:
Think FAST
• Face droop
• Arm weakness
• Speech difficulty
• Time matters

First test
• Non-contrast CT head

If ischemic stroke
• Consider thrombolysis if within window and eligible

If hemorrhagic stroke
• No thrombolysis
• Neurosurgery if indicated

High-yield rule
• Always exclude bleed first

title: 🫁 PULMONARY EMBOLISM
content:
When to suspect PE
• Sudden dyspnea
• Pleuritic chest pain
• Tachycardia
• Hypoxia
• Risk factors such as immobility, cancer, recent surgery, pregnancy

Diagnosis
• Clinical probability first
• D-dimer in low or intermediate risk
• CT pulmonary angiography if indicated

Treatment
• Anticoagulation if stable
• Thrombolysis if unstable massive PE

High-yield clue
• Sudden unexplained shortness of breath + pleuritic pain

title: 🌬️ TENSION PNEUMOTHORAX
content:
Classic findings
• Sudden dyspnea
• Hypotension
• Tracheal deviation
• Reduced breath sounds on one side
• Distended neck veins may be present

Immediate treatment
• Needle decompression first
• Chest tube after

High-yield rule
• Treat immediately if clinically obvious
• Do not wait for X-ray

title: 🍬 DKA VS HYPOGLYCEMIA
content:
🔴 DKA
• Hyperglycemia
• Ketones
• Metabolic acidosis
• Dehydration
• Kussmaul breathing

Treatment
• IV fluids
• IV insulin
• Monitor potassium closely

🟢 Hypoglycemia
• Sweating
• Tremor
• Confusion
• Seizures may occur

Treatment
• Glucose immediately
• IV dextrose if severe

High-yield shortcut
• DKA = high sugar + acid
• Hypoglycemia = low sugar + neuroglycopenia

title: 🩻 TRAUMA PRIMARY SURVEY
content:
Always do ABCDE
• A with cervical spine protection
• B assess chest rise and breath sounds
• C control bleeding and assess perfusion
• D assess GCS and pupils
• E expose fully and prevent hypothermia

Life-threatening chest injuries to remember
• Tension pneumothorax
• Massive hemothorax
• Cardiac tamponade

FAST ultrasound
• Detects internal bleeding quickly

title: ☠️ HIGH-YIELD ANTIDOTES
content:
Opioids → Naloxone
Benzodiazepines → Flumazenil
Acetaminophen → N-acetylcysteine
Organophosphates → Atropine ± pralidoxime
Methanol / Ethylene glycol → Fomepizole
Anaphylaxis → Epinephrine

High-yield exam strategy
• Always connect the toxidrome to the antidote
